Petition For Order Approving Modification Of Trust Terms On Consent Of All Beneficiaries And Instructions For Trustee To Make Periodic Distributions Of Trust Assets (Probate Code 15403, 17200(B)(13), And 17200(B)(6))
Off Calendar Nov-15-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Evan Abrams, via Zoom, for City National Bank, Petitioner.

The court confirms with counsel that the 14th accounting previously settled by the court will likely be the final accounting if the instant petition is granted. The court advises that the court will review the proposed order and make some adjustments.

The matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Petition For Order Approving Modification Of Trust Terms On Consent Of All Beneficiaries And Instructions For Trustee To Make Periodic Distributions Of Trust Assets (Probate Code 15403, 17200(B)(13), And 17200(B)(6))
Off Calendar Oct-25-2022 Continued To Nov-14-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #14, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Evan Abrams appeared by telephone for City National Bank (Trustee).

Mr. Abrams stated that they are happy to comply with whatever the court's preference is. The court noted that this is a type of trust that wouldn't be set up today, that it's a trust that worked its way from Contra Costa County, that the beneficiary lives in Pennsylvania and that Counsel presented the beneficiary's consent. The court also asked about the beneficiary's capacity and ability to manage his finances. Mr. Abrams stated that it is his understanding that the beneficiary has limited capacity/a mental capacity of a 14-year-old. The Fourteenth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission with the court's intent to grant the petition. The court informed counsel that it is open to changes in this if desired by the trustee, or the beneficiary by way of taking the trust out of court supervision, or modification of the terms.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #14, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Aug-23-2021 Continued To Sep-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #13, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #12, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-26-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Weaver for City National Bank (Petitioner/Trustee).

Mr. Weaver stated that under the Trust there is wide latitude for the Trustee to make distribution to promote the general welfare of the Beneficiary, that the income is more than double the expenses, that the Trustee made a request to this Court at the last accounting to have authority to distribute net income to the parents and that if the funds were trapped in the Trust, there would be no gain. Mr. Weaver also stated that expenses went down, that the corpus is nearly $15,000,000.00, that the Father is no longer working/retired at age 55, that the Father is spending time with the family/his son and that it has been a blessing. In addition, Mr. Weaver spoke of the tax rate with the money remaining in the Trust, stated that City National would charge fees on those funds, that the parents are the contingent remainder beneficiaries and that some of the money is going to pay for college for the other boys. The Court informed Counsel that it is reducing the monthly amount to $20,000.00. The Twelfth Account and Report, etc. is taken under submission for the Court to modify the order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING , REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-07-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lara Gilman for City National Bank (Trustee). Michael Korbholz also present.

Ms. Gilman presented oral arguemnt to support her position with regard to the Parents being repaid for prior Court ordered reimbursement from Michael's Parents to the Trust, increase stipend, vacation money, the purchase of an auto dealership, and the hourly rate of the Attorney and the Paralegal. Upon conclusion of arguemnt, the Court granted an increase in the monthly stipend from $2,000.00 to $4,000.00, denied the request for repayment to Michael's Parents for the amount of reimbursment to the Trust as ordered in a prior Court order, allowed $40,000.00 for vacation money stating that anything beyond that amount would be subject to court approval, allowed the order to allow the father to buy the car dealership to be in place for one more year from today stating that a petition would need to be filed therafter and took the fee issue under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #8, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-24-2009 Continued To Sep-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Korbholz for City National Bank (Trustee).

Mr. Korbholz stated that the vehicles were purchased by the Parents, that there would be a refund for 25% of the insurance premiums paid and that the vacations for the whole family were paid from the trust otherwise the family could not afford it. The Court stated that the vacations needed to reflect the costs for Michael only and that reimbursement may be required. The Court directed Mr. Korbholz to file a declaration by September 4, 2009. The matter was continued to September 14, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR#7077. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
